stocks.recent_news: Calendar-year reporter. Q1 FY27 unaudited results scheduled April 29, 2026; earnings call April 30 at 10:30 IST (MD Harsha Kadam, CFO Hardevi Vazirani). Just went ex-dividend April 23, 2026 for Rs 35/share final dividend (1,750% on face value Rs 2) - highest in 5 years. Stock rose 6.1% to Rs 4,332 post-Q4 FY26 results; trading Rs 4,288 on April 22, market cap ~Rs 65,224 cr. Up 8% in last month, 10% YTD, ~30% YoY, 330% over 5 years. Marketsmojo upgraded from Hold to Buy citing strong technicals, debt-free status. Golden crossover technical signal flagged.
stocks.sector_context: Nifty Auto at 26,522 (April 20). India bearings market USD 5.2B in 2025, projected ~9.7% CAGR through 2034; auto bearings ~60% of demand. Tailwinds from EV/hybrid drivetrain demand, smart-bearings adoption, and government 'Bharat Bearing Program' (May 2025). Listed bearings makers (Schaeffler, SKF India, Timken India) saw FY26/FY27 earnings upgrades on industrial + auto demand recovery. Headwind: steel/alloy raw-material cost volatility.
stocks.peer_comparison: Schaeffler ~16% YoY revenue growth leads bearings peer group. Trades at premium vs SKF India and Timken India, supported by industrial diversification (not pure auto). Revenue growth above Bosch India. Debt-free balance sheet superior to most peers.
red_flags: Just went ex-dividend April 23 - mechanical price drop today
Q1 FY27 results April 29 are binary event
Premium valuation vs SKF/Timken peers
No ASM/GSM inclusion detected
catalysts: Q1 FY27 unaudited results April 29, 2026 + earnings call April 30
Management guidance on industrial vs auto mix
Golden crossover may attract momentum flows

stocks.recent_news: Q4 FY26 results expected April 28, 2026. Q3 FY26 baseline strong: net profit up 16.9% YoY to Rs 680 cr, revenue up 9.5% YoY to Rs 4,885 cr. Stock at Rs 5,729 on April 22, 2026; vs 52-week range Rs 4,600-6,300 (~9% below high). Analysts project Q4 PAT Rs 560-610 cr, revenue Rs 4,300-4,600 cr; volume growth guidance 8-12% YoY.
stocks.sector_context: FMCG sector expected to deliver 6-8% revenue growth in FY26 (100-200 bps recovery). Q4 FY26 outlook healthy on post-GST volume recovery, easing agri-input costs, and rural demand revival. Rural growth (6.7%) outpacing urban (4.9%). Margin pressure from input costs remains a watchpoint.
stocks.peer_comparison: Britannia tracks well vs Nestle, HUL, Marico. Biscuits leadership intact; volume growth guidance ahead of broader FMCG average. Premium valuation justified by margin profile and brand moat.
red_flags: Stock 9% below 52-week high suggests near-term softness or expectations reset
Input-cost margin pressure remains a sector-wide drag
No ASM/GSM inclusion detected
catalysts: Q4 FY26 results on April 28, 2026 - imminent binary event
Rural demand rebound commentary on call

stocks.recent_news: Board met today (April 24, 2026) to approve Q4 FY26 + FY26 audited results plus FY26 dividend (first in nearly 2 years). Stock Rs 867 (down 11% from 52W high Rs 968). Net advances fell 8.7% YoY to Rs 3,15,154 cr (sequential -0.8%); Q3 FY26 PAT had collapsed 91%. Analyst Q4 expectations: NII Rs 4,646 cr (+52% YoY), PAT Rs 822-1,600 cr. Promoters pledged 6.45% stake (5.02 cr shares) to refinance USD 1.445B debt - fresh pledge creation. Bank seeking to offload Rs 1,573 cr of stressed microfinance loans. Jagdeep Mallareddy appointed Head-Consumer Banking (April 9). CARE Ratings reaffirmed CARE A1+ on CDs (April 8).
stocks.sector_context: Private banks under pressure from ICRA note flagging steeper FY27 NPA rise vs PSBs. Microfinance segment under sector-wide stress. Macquarie: IndusInd credit cost stuck at ~140 bps vs 50-60 bps for HDFC/ICICI peers. Bajaj Broking flagged INDUSINDBK as April 2026 top buy on potential re-rating if asset quality normalizes - contrarian view.
stocks.peer_comparison: HDFC Bank GNPA 1.42% and ICICI Bank GNPA 1.40% (Q4 FY26) contrast with IndusInd's MFI-driven stress. Trades at meaningful P/B discount. Analyst ratings split: Elara Reduce (TP Rs 910), JM Financial Reduce (TP Rs 780), Motilal Oswal Neutral (TP Rs 930), YES Securities Buy (TP Rs 1,000). MarketsMojo recently downgraded to Sell.
red_flags: SFIO investigation initiated December 2025 over Rs 1,979 cr accounting lapse
SEBI ex-parte interim order (May 2025) barred 5 former senior executives incl ex-CEO Sumant Kathpalia and Deputy CEO Arun Khurana; impounded Rs 19.8 cr for insider trading on UPSI
Cumulative microfinance discrepancies: Rs 173 cr + Rs 674 cr previously disclosed
Promoters pledged 6.45% stake for USD 1.445B refinancing - fresh pledge creation
Net advances declined 8.7% YoY in Q4 FY26 - rare among private banks
MarketsMojo Sell downgrade; Elara/JM Financial Reduce ratings
Q3 FY26 PAT collapsed 91% YoY - Q4 recovery uncertain
catalysts: Q4 FY26 results + FY26 dividend announcement TODAY (April 24, 2026)
First dividend in ~2 years would be confidence-restoring
Asset-quality normalization signals could trigger sharp re-rating
Rs 1,573 cr microfinance loan-sale closure clarity

stocks.recent_news: Board meeting April 30, 2026 to consider FY26 audited results, dividend, AND first-ever share buyback - major positive signal. Stock has rallied 34.7% in last month, 47.4% YoY on buyback anticipation. Closed Rs 1,200, market cap ~Rs 19,113 cr.
stocks.sector_context: India ceramic tiles market USD 10.45B in 2025, growing 8.4% CAGR to USD 15.84B by 2030. Residential segment 67% share with 8.93% CAGR. PMAY-U 2.0 (10 mn pucca homes, Rs 10 lakh cr commitment) creates strong order pipeline. Replacement/renovation cycle accelerating.
stocks.peer_comparison: Sector leader; first-mover on buyback signals confidence vs Somany Ceramics, Asian Granito, Cera Sanitaryware. Strong rally outpacing sector peers.
red_flags: Stock has run up 34% in a month - much positive news already priced in; 'sell the news' risk post-buyback announcement
Volume recovery in tiles segment still being awaited per analysts
Buyback price below current market would disappoint
No ASM/GSM inclusion detected
catalysts: Board meeting April 30 - buyback approval + Q4/FY26 results + dividend - triple catalyst
Buyback price will be key for sentiment continuation

stocks.recent_news: Q4 FY26 results posted April 23, 2026: consolidated PAT up 5.66% YoY to Rs 683.78 cr. FY26 revenue Rs 18,296 cr, PAT Rs 2,393 cr, EBITDA Rs 8,726 cr. Mumbai HVDC commissioned. Crossed 1 crore smart meters - first in India globally; targeting another 1 crore in coming year. Auditors issued unmodified opinion. Earnings call TODAY (April 24) at 11:00 IST. Stock at Rs 1,361 (April 23), market cap Rs 1,63,999 cr. AGM scheduled June 25, 2026.
stocks.sector_context: Power transmission/T&D sector benefits from grid capex push, renewables integration, and smart-metering rollout. AESL is dominant private transmission player. PowerGrid is the listed PSU benchmark.
stocks.peer_comparison: Limited direct listed peers in pure transmission. Trades at premium to PowerGrid on growth profile but with Adani-group governance discount overhang. Smart-meter scale leadership a structural moat.
red_flags: Adani group governance overhang persists - February 2026 OCCRP report alleged ~USD 3 bn secret investments by Adani family associates (Chang Chung-Ling, Nasser Ali Shaban Ahli)
Swiss criminal investigation underway re: money laundering and document forgery linked to these individuals
Multiple SEBI investigations remain pending though Sept 2025 saw two 'final orders' clearing specific Hindenburg allegations
PAT growth modest at 5.66% YoY - below historical run-rate
Group concentration risk: any adverse Adani-group news drags AESL
catalysts: Earnings call TODAY April 24, 2026 at 11:00 IST - management guidance key
